zh.Pengwern, Llangollen, Denbighshire, Wale: 英國威爾斯丹比爾利朗勒朋維
zh.lifeSummary: 當Dows verch Richard Hywel Mostyn於1505年出生在英國威爾斯丹比爾利朗勒朋維時，她的父親Lord Richard ap Howel of Mostyn35歲，母親Catrin Salusbury36歲。她於1528年與Thomas Griffith Fychan在英國威爾斯福林爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1560年在英國威爾斯丹比爾享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dows verch Richard Hywel Mostyn于1505年出生在英国威尔斯丹比爾利朗勒朋維时，她的父亲Lord Richard ap Howel of Mostyn35岁，母亲Catrin Salusbury36岁。她于1528年与Thomas Griffith Fychan在英国威尔斯福林爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1560年在英国威尔斯丹比爾享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
meanings: A Welsh name. Some write the name Hoel, which they render 'lordly;' if so, it may come from huail, a vice-regent, regent. Arthur derives the surname Howell from Corn. -Brit, houl, the sun; and he adds, high, exalted.' But Hywel is rather from hywel, conspicuous.

nameMeanings.name: Mostyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: habitational name from Mostyn in Flintshire, Wales, the name of which is derived from Old English mos ‘bog’ + tūn ‘farm, village’.
History: This surname was adopted by a 16th-century Lord of Mostyn, Thomas ap Richard ap Hywel, and his brother Piers.

variants.name: Mustain
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of Welsh and English   Mostyn  . Compare   Mustian  .

nameMeanings.name: Dows
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of West Frisian and Dutch   Douwes  .

variants.name: Douwes
meanings: West Frisian and Dutch: genitivized patronymic from the personal name Douwe, from Frisian dou ‘pigeon’.

nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Dows verch Richard Hywel Mostyn was born in 1505, in Pengwern, Llangollen, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Lord Richard ap Howel of Mostyn, was 35 and her mother, Catrin Salusbury, was 36. She married Thomas Griffith Fychan in 1528, in Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1560, in Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

Elle a épousé Thomas Griffith Fychan en 1528, à Flintshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1560, à Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans.

Sie hat 1528 in Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom Thomas Griffith Fychan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1560 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Dows verch Richard Hywel Mostynが1505年にイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアランゴレンペングウエルンで生まれた時，父Lord Richard ap Howel of Mostynは35歳，母Catrin Salusburyは36歳であった。1528年にイギリスウェールズフリントシュアイアでThomas Griffith Fychanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1560年にイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dows verch Richard Hywel Mostyn(이)가 1505년 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 랭렌 펜그워에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Richard ap Howel of Mostyn(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catrin Salusbury(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1528년 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르에서 Thomas Griffith Fychan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1560년 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dows verch Richard Hywel Mostyn родилась в 1505 г. в местности пэнгвирн, лланголлен, денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Lord Richard ap Howel of Mostyn было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Catrin Salusbury, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Griffith Fychan в 1528 г. в местности флинцхир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1560 г. в местности денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет.
